etymology.description: semantic 艹 (grass) + phonetic 樂 (sound) -> medicine
etymology.interpretation: The character 藥 (yào, "medicine") combines the semantic 艹 (cǎo, "grass") indicating herbs, traditionally used for medicinal purposes, with the phonetic 樂 (lè, "music") contributing the sound. This suggests the harmonious preparation of herbal remedies.
etymology.simplification: 藥 simplified to 药 with the 艹 (艹) component retained and simplification of the phonetic 樂 (樂) to simplify writing.

examples.chinese: 医生给我开了药(醫生給我開了藥)。
examples.english: The doctor prescribed medicine for me.

examples.chinese: 如果你不舒服(如果你不舒服)，记得吃药(記得吃藥)。
examples.pinyin: Rúguǒ nǐ bù shūfú, jìdé chī yào.
examples.english: If you're not feeling well, remember to take medicine.

examples.chinese: 这药很苦(這藥很苦)，但很有效(但很有效)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè yào hěn kǔ, dàn hěn yǒuxiào.
examples.english: This medicine is bitter but very effective.
